findings.message: New dependency `requests` is added unpinned (no version constraint), unlike every other dependency in this manifest which uses a lower bound (e.g. `>=2.x`). Unpinned deps make resolution non-reproducible and can silently introduce major-version breakage.
findings.suggestion: Pin to a supported lower bound, e.g. `requests>=2.32`.

findings.message: `requests` duplicates capability already available via `httpx` (listed in dev deps and a transitive of the FastAPI/Starlette stack). `httpx` is actively developed, supports sync and async, HTTP/2, and integrates with this project's async FastAPI runtime; `requests` is in maintenance-only mode and is sync-only, which can block the event loop if called from async handlers.
findings.suggestion: Drop `requests` and use `httpx` (promote it from dev to runtime deps if needed) for outbound HTTP calls.

findings.message: Supply-chain note: adding a runtime HTTP client expands the attack surface. `requests` is widely used and reasonably maintained, but if kept, ensure `pip-audit` (already in dev deps) is run in CI to catch CVEs against its transitive chain (urllib3, charset-normalizer, certifi).
findings.suggestion: Confirm `pip-audit` runs in the project's CI workflow and pin `requests` so audit results are reproducible.
